title: Mediolobivia sarothroides
taxonomy: Mediolobivia sarothroides is a species of cactus that belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. It is classified within the family Cactaceae. This species is further classified as part of the genus Mediolobivia, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. Mediolobivia sarothroides is native to Argentina and is a member of the tribe Cacteae. The classification of Mediolobivia sarothroides reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Mediolobivia sarothroides, also known as the "Night-Blooming Cereus" or "Mexican Evening Star," is a species of cactus belonging to the genus Mediolobivia, characterized by its nocturnal blooming habit and vibrant, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a possible treatment for various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Mediolobivia sarothroides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment to harness its health benefits, with the most common preparations being used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and fever.
